questions.content:
Let $y = \dfrac{x^{2} - x}{e^{x}}$.

Find and simplify $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$.

Let $f(x) = \sin(x)e^{2x}$.

Find $f'\!\left(\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)$.
Solve $e^{2x} - 12 = 4e^{x}$ for $x \in R$.
Sketch the graph of $f(x) = 2 - \dfrac{3}{x - 1}$ on the axes below, labelling all asymptotes with their equations and axial intercepts with their coordinates.
Find the values of $x$ for which $f(x) \leq 1$.

The graph of $y = x + \dfrac{1}{x}$ is shown over part of its domain.

Use two trapeziums of equal width to approximate the area between the curve, the $x$-axis and the lines $x = 1$ and $x = 3$.
questions.imageDescription: Graph of y = x + 1/x shown for x > 0, displaying a curve with a minimum near x = 1, increasing for larger x values.

questions.content: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \sin(x)\,dx$.
Hence, or otherwise, find all values of $k$ such that $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \sin(x)\,dx = \int_{k}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos(x)\,dx$, where $-3\pi < k < 2\pi$.

Let $\hat{P}$ be the random variable that represents the sample proportion of households in a given suburb that have solar panels installed.

From a sample of randomly selected households in a given suburb, an approximate 95% confidence interval for the proportion $p$ of households having solar panels installed was determined to be $(0.04, 0.16)$.

Find the value of $\hat{p}$ that was used to obtain this approximate 95% confidence interval.

Use $z = 2$ to approximate the 95% confidence interval.

Find the size of the sample from which this 95% confidence interval was obtained.

A larger sample of households is selected, with a sample size four times the original sample. The sample proportion of households having solar panels installed is found to be the same.

By what factor will the increased sample size affect the width of the confidence interval?

Consider $f: (-\infty, 1] \to R$, $f(x) = x^{2} - 2x$. Part of the graph of $y = f(x)$ is shown below.

State the range of $f$.
questions.imageDescription: Graph of f(x) = x^2 - 2x for x <= 1, showing a parabola with vertex at (1, -1) marked with a closed dot, decreasing from top-left.

questions.content: Sketch the graph of the inverse function $y = f^{-1}(x)$ on the axes above. Label any endpoints and axial intercepts with their coordinates.
Determine the equation and the domain for the inverse function $f^{-1}$.
Calculate the area of the regions enclosed by the curves of $f$, $f^{-1}$ and $y = -x$.

Suppose that the queuing time, $T$ (in minutes), at a customer service desk has a probability density function given by
$$f(t) = \begin{cases} kt(16 - t^{2}) & 0 \leq t \leq 4 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$$
for some $k \in R$.

Show that $k = \dfrac{1}{64}$.
Find $\text{E}(T)$.
What is the probability that a person has to queue for more than two minutes, given that they have already queued for one minute?

The shapes of two walking tracks are shown below.

Track 1 is described by the function $f(x) = a - x(x - 2)^{2}$.
Track 2 is defined by the function $g(x) = 12x + bx^{2}$.
The unit of length is kilometres.

Given that $f(0) = 12$ and $g(1) = 9$, verify that $a = 12$ and $b = -3$.
questions.imageDescription: Graph showing two walking tracks: Track 1 (solid curve) starting high on the y-axis with a local maximum at point P, and Track 2 (dashed curve) starting at the origin and also reaching point P.

questions.content: Verify that $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ both have a turning point at $P$. Give the co-ordinates of $P$.

A theme park is planned whose boundaries will form the triangle $\triangle OAB$ where $O$ is the origin, $A$ is at $(k, 0)$ and $B$ is at $(k, g(k))$, as shown below, where $k \in (0, 4)$.

Find the maximum possible area of the theme park, in km$^{2}$.
questions.imageDescription: Graph showing both tracks with the triangle OAB highlighted, where O is the origin, A is on the x-axis, and B is on track 2 at x = k.
